avatarUpload.title: AvatarUpload
avatarUpload.description: An all-in-one avatar manager: upload a photo, crop it to a circle, and optionally generate one with AI. The AI provider is always external — you supply the function; mood-ui only handles the UI and state.

basic.title: Basic upload
basic.desc: Drop or click to pick a file. The built-in circular cropper opens automatically before committing the image.

noCrop.title: Without crop step
noCrop.desc: Set :crop="false" to skip the cropper and use the resized image directly.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.desc: Three sizes control the preview circle and icon scale.

saveMode.title: Save mode — manual
saveMode.desc: With saveMode="manual" a Save button appears once an image is ready. The onSave callback receives an AvatarSavePayload so you can upload to your backend and return the final URL.

withAi.title: AI generation (mock)
withAi.desc: Provide a :provider function that calls your own AI API and returns a URL. mood-ui never imports AI SDKs directly.

lightbox.title: Lightbox preview
lightbox.desc: Add :lightbox="true" to show an expand button on the avatar. Clicking it opens the image full-size in a modal — useful in profile cards, read-only views, and dashboards.

profileCard.title: Profile card
profileCard.desc: Horizontal layout with the avatar on the left and user info on the right — a common pattern in dashboards and profile pages.

settingsRow.title: Settings row
settingsRow.desc: Compact horizontal row used in account settings: avatar alongside a short description and the upload controls below.

inForm.title: Inside a form
inForm.desc: Avatar centered at the top of a profile form, with name and bio fields below — a typical onboarding pattern.

readonly.title: Read-only
readonly.desc: Displays the avatar without any upload or action controls.

props.lightbox: Show an expand button on hover. Clicking it opens the image full-size in a modal.
props.promptSuggestions: Quick-pick prompt chips shown above the text input. Selecting a chip sets the prompt; selecting it again clears it.
props.ai: Enable AI generation UI. Requires a provider function.
props.provider: Function called by mood-ui to generate an avatar. Receives \{ prompt, image, variant \} and an AbortSignal. Returns a Promise(string) (URL).
props.variant: Initial selected style variant for AI generation.
props.name: User name used as alt text and avatar initials fallback.
props.size: Size of the preview circle and icons.
props.crop: Open the circular crop modal after file selection. Default: true.
props.maxSize: Maximum file size in bytes. Default: 5 MB.
props.accept: Allowed MIME types. Default: PNG, JPEG, WebP.
props.regenerate: Show regenerate button after first AI generation.
props.promptPlaceholder: Custom placeholder for the prompt input.
props.saveMode: 'auto' calls onSave immediately. 'manual' shows a Save button. 'none' emits update:modelValue with the blob URL directly.
props.onSave: Async function that receives AvatarSavePayload and returns the final URL after uploading to your backend.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted with the final URL whenever the avatar changes.
emits.generated: Emitted with the URL returned by the provider after successful generation.
emits.uploaded: Emitted with the original File object after upload validation.
emits.error: Emitted with the Error on any failure (validation, generation, save).
emits.loading: Emitted with the loading boolean state.
emits.change: Emitted with the current URL or null whenever the preview changes.
emits.remove: Emitted when the avatar is removed.
emits.save: Emitted with AvatarSavePayload just before the onSave call.
emits.saved: Emitted with the final URL after onSave resolves.
emits.saveError: Emitted with the Error if onSave rejects.

integration.title: AI provider integration
integration.desc: mood-ui never ships an AI SDK — you bring your own. The component calls the provider function you supply; that function can call any API you want. The cleanest approach is a backend endpoint that holds your API keys.
integration.helperDesc: The included helper turns a URL into a ready-to-use provider. By default it POSTs \{ prompt, variant \} and auto-detects common response shapes (OpenAI, Replicate, or plain \{ url \}).
integration.backendNodeDesc: Create a POST endpoint that calls your AI service and returns \{ url \}. mood-ui fetches that URL and converts it to a local blob.
integration.uploadTitle: Saving the processed image to your backend
integration.uploadDesc: When onSave is provided, mood-ui calls it with an AvatarSavePayload containing a 512×512 WebP Blob. Upload it to S3, Cloudinary, Supabase Storage, etc. and return the final CDN URL.
integration.directTitle: Direct AI API calls (development / testing only)
integration.directDesc: You can call an AI API directly from the browser during development. Never do this in production — API keys in the frontend are public. Use a .env variable and exclude it from production builds.

types.title: Types
types.desc: TypeScript interfaces for AvatarUpload props, the provider function, and the save payload.

a11y.kbActivate: Activate the upload control or confirm action in a dialog
a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.kbEsc: Close the crop modal or AI panel, restore focus to the trigger
a11y.ariaRole: Upload zone is exposed as a button so screen readers announce it as interactive
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name for the upload control when the user name is not set
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.alt: The user name prop is used as alt text on the avatar image for screen readers
a11y.ariaLive: Loading and error states are announced to screen readers via a polite live region
a11y.focusUpload: The upload zone is focusable and participates in the tab order as a native button
a11y.focusCrop: The crop modal traps focus while open; Tab cycles within the dialog
a11y.focusTrap: Focus returns to the upload trigger when the crop modal or AI panel is closed
a11y.focusDisabled: Excluded from the tab order when the disabled prop is true

button.title: Button
button.description: The primary action of any interface. Four variants, five semantic colors, four sizes, configurable icons, loading state and support for rendering as a link or any element.

variants.title: Variants
variants.desc: Four visual styles for different levels of action hierarchy in the interface.

colors.title: Colors
colors.desc: Five semantic colors that map directly to the ModoProvider design token system.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.desc: Padding, font and height scale proportionally at each level.

icons.title: With icons
icons.desc: Left, right or icon-only position. Use aria-label on icon-only buttons.

states.title: States
states.desc: Loading blocks clicks and shows spinner. Disabled prevents all interaction.

gradient.title: Gradient
gradient.desc: Gradient fill for a more premium visual look on supported colors.

radius.title: Radius
radius.desc: Corner radius from sharp edges to fully rounded pills.

link.title: As link
link.desc: Render as an anchor tag while keeping button styling. Useful for navigation that looks like a CTA.

skeleton.title: Skeleton
skeleton.desc: Show a skeleton placeholder with the same footprint while data or context is loading.

props.variant: Visual variant. Normal is the solid button (default).
props.color: Semantic color applied to the button.
props.size: Controls padding, font size and height.
props.radius: Corner radius. Overrides the active radius from ModoProvider when set.
props.label: Button text. Equivalent to the default slot for plain content.
props.type: HTML button type attribute. Use 'submit' inside forms.
props.loading: Shows a spinner and blocks clicks while active.
props.loadingText: Text shown alongside the spinner during loading state.
props.disabled: Visually disables the button and prevents all interaction.
props.icon: Icon component rendered next to the label (heroicons or any component).
props.iconPosition: Side where the icon appears relative to the label.
props.fullWidth: Button expands to fill 100% of available width.
props.gradient: Apply a gradient background on supported colors.
props.skeleton: Render a skeleton placeholder instead of the real button.
props.as: Root element to render. Use "a" for links or a router-link component.
props.ariaLabel: Accessible label. Required for icon-only buttons without visible text.

emits.click: Emitted on click when the button is not disabled or in loading state.

slots.default: Custom button content. Replaces the label prop when HTML or inner components are needed.

types.title: Button
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the Button component props. All props are optional and have sensible defaults.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.ariaType: Prevents accidental form submission when the button is inside a form element
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaBusy: Communicates loading state while the action is in progress
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name for icon-only buttons (no visible text)
a11y.focusNative: Uses a native button element — participates in the tab order by default
a11y.focusDisabled: Excluded from the tab order when the disabled prop is true
a11y.focusRing: Focus ring is always visible; style is controlled by the focusHalo setting in ModoConfig

buttonGroup.title: ButtonGroup
buttonGroup.description: Groups several buttons into a single visual unit sharing variant, color and size. Supports horizontal and vertical orientation.

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.desc: Three grouped buttons sharing color and variant.

mixed.title: Mixed colors
mixed.desc: Each button can override the group color to highlight specific actions.

fullWidth.title: Full width
fullWidth.desc: The group expands to 100% and buttons share the width proportionally.

vertical.title: Vertical
vertical.desc: Stacks buttons in a column — useful for context menus or action lists.

icons.title: With icons
icons.desc: Icon-only grouped buttons for compact toolbars.

props.orientation: Axis along which the buttons are stacked.
props.variant: Visual variant inherited by all buttons in the group.
props.color: Semantic color applied to the buttons in the group.
props.size: Size shared by all buttons in the group.
props.radius: Border radius applied to the wrapper. Inner button corners collapse automatically.
props.disabled: Disables all buttons in the group.
props.gradient: Applies a subtle gradient compatible with the normal variant.
props.fullWidth: Expands the group and all buttons to fill the available width.

slots.default: List of Button components that belong to the group.

types.title: ButtonGroup
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the ButtonGroup component props. Props are inherited by child buttons unless they override them individually.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element within the group
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element within the group
a11y.ariaGroup: The wrapper is exposed as a group so screen readers recognize it as a related set of buttons
a11y.ariaLabel: Provide an accessible name when the group lacks a visible text label
a11y.ariaDisabled: When set on the group, aria-disabled is forwarded to each child button
a11y.focusNoTrap: Focus moves freely between buttons; no focus trap is applied at the group level
a11y.focusDisabled: All child buttons are excluded from the tab order when the disabled prop is true

checkbox.title: Checkbox
checkbox.description: Accessible checkbox with support for indeterminate state, description and all color and size variants.

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.desc: Controlled checkbox with v-model.

description.title: With description
description.desc: Secondary text below the label to clarify the purpose.
description.label: Notifications
description.helpText: Receive email alerts when there is activity.

indeterminate.title: Indeterminate
indeterminate.desc: Useful for representing partial selection in lists with "select all".
indeterminate.label: Partial selection

colors.title: Colors
colors.desc: Semantic variants for the checked state.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.desc: Three sizes to match form density.

disabled.title: Disabled
disabled.desc: Disabled state preserving the visual value.

props.label: Visible label to the right of the control.
props.description: Secondary descriptive text below the label.
props.helperText: Helper text below the control. Hidden when errorText is present.
props.errorText: Error message that replaces helperText and applies an error state.
props.color: Semantic color applied in the checked state.
props.size: Size of the box and label.
props.indeterminate: Tri-state: shows a dash instead of the checkmark.
props.disabled: Disables the checkbox and prevents interaction.
props.readonly: Blocks changes but keeps focus and active styles.
props.required: Marks the field as required and shows an asterisk.
props.name: HTML name attribute for form submission.
props.value: HTML value attribute, useful inside a checkbox group.
props.ariaLabel: Accessible name when there is no visible label.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted when the value changes (syncs v-model).

types.title: Checkbox
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the Checkbox component props. Supports tri-state with indeterminate, validation helpers, and full theming.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.ariaChecked: Communicates checked, unchecked or indeterminate state
a11y.ariaInvalid: Set to true when errorText is present
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links helper or error text to the checkbox for screen reader context
a11y.ariaRequired: Set to true when the required prop is active
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name when no visible label is present
a11y.focusNative: Uses a native input[type=checkbox] — participates in the tab order by default
a11y.focusIndeterminate: The indeterminate state is visually reflected and announced to screen readers

colorModeSwitch.title: ColorModeSwitch
colorModeSwitch.description: Three-state segmented selector for the color mode (light · system · dark) with a smooth animated sliding indicator. Supports a collapsed variant that expands on hover.

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.desc: Bind the switch to the useColorMode composable to control the theme globally.

collapsed.title: Collapsed variant
collapsed.desc: Shows only the active mode at rest. On hover it smoothly expands to reveal all three options.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.desc: Three sizes available for different UI densities.

withLabels.title: With text labels
withLabels.desc: Enable showLabels to display the mode name alongside the icon.

props.variant: Control appearance. 'default' always shows all three options; 'collapsed' shows only the active one and expands on hover.
props.ariaLabel: Accessible name for the radiogroup. Defaults to 'Color mode'.
props.showLabels: Shows text labels next to each icon.
props.disabled: Disables the control and prevents selection.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted when a mode is selected (syncs v-model).

a11y.kbTab: Move focus into or out of the color mode group
a11y.kbArrow: Move selection between light / system / dark options
a11y.ariaGroup: The wrapper is exposed as a radiogroup for screen readers
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name for the radiogroup
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.focusGroup: Arrow keys cycle between modes; Tab moves focus out of the group
a11y.focusCollapsed: In collapsed variant, focus expands the control automatically

colorPicker.title: ColorPicker
colorPicker.description: Color selector with popover, configurable swatches, hex input and advanced HSV canvas for picking any color.

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.desc: Swatches only. Click any color to select it.

advanced.title: Advanced picker
advanced.desc: HSV canvas + hue slider to choose any color in the spectrum. The hex input allows entering exact values.

swatches.title: Swatches + advanced picker
swatches.desc: Combines a predefined palette with the full canvas for maximum flexibility.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.desc: Three available sizes to match the density of your form.

disabled.title: Disabled
disabled.desc: Disabled state preserving the visible color.

props.swatches: Optional list of hex colors available as preset swatches in the panel.
props.showHex: Shows the hex input below the swatches.
props.advanced: Enables the HSV canvas + hue slider for picking any color.
props.variant: Visual variant of the trigger (same as Input/Select).
props.color: Focus ring color.
props.radius: Border radius of the trigger and the panel.
props.label: Visible label above the trigger.
props.placement: Position of the popover relative to the trigger.
props.ariaLabel: Accessible name for the trigger when there is no visible label.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted when the selected color changes (syncs v-model).
emits.change: Emitted after a color is confirmed by the user.

types.title: ColorPicker
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the ColorPicker component props. Supports preset swatches, hex input, and advanced HSV canvas picking.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.kbEsc: Close the color picker popover without selecting
a11y.kbArrow: Navigate swatches or adjust hue/saturation in advanced mode
a11y.ariaExpanded: Communicates whether the popover is open or closed
a11y.ariaInvalid: Set to true when errorText is present
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links helper or error text to the trigger for screen reader context
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name when no visible label is present
a11y.focusNative: Uses a native button as trigger — participates in the tab order by default
a11y.focusTrap: Focus is trapped inside the popover while it is open

combobox.title: Combobox
combobox.description: Autocomplete text input with static or async suggestions. Supports free values (combobox) or strict selection (autocomplete).

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.desc: Static suggestions shown as the user types.

freeSolo.title: Free solo (creatable)
freeSolo.desc: Type a new value and press Enter. The dropdown stays open so you can see the option being added to the list. The \@change handler detects unknown values and pushes them into the reactive options array.

strict.title: Strict (autocomplete)
strict.desc: With free-solo set to false, only values present in options are accepted.

async.title: Async search
async.desc: Pass loadOptions to resolve options from a backend with debounce.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.desc: Three sizes for different UI densities.

props.options: Static options list. Ignored when loadOptions is defined.
props.loadOptions: Async options source called on every query change.
props.debounce: Delay in ms before calling loadOptions.
props.minChars: Minimum characters before triggering the async search.
props.freeSolo: Allows any text as value. If false, only valid options are accepted.
props.label: Visible label above the field.
props.errorText: Error message that replaces the helperText.
props.emptyText: Text shown when there are no options to display.
props.loadingText: Text shown while the async search is in progress.
props.color: Semantic color for focus and accent.
props.clearable: Shows an X button to clear the value.
props.fullWidth: Applies w-full to the root control.
props.iconLeft: Icon on the left side of the input.
props.name: HTML name attribute for form submission.
props.ariaLabel: Accessible name when there is no visible label.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted when the input value changes.
emits.change: Emitted after selecting an option or confirming text.
emits.select: Emitted when an option is chosen from the list.

types.title: Combobox
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the Combobox component props. Uses SelectOption for its options array. See the Select page for the option shape.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.kbType: Filter the option list by typing
a11y.kbEsc: Close the dropdown panel without selecting
a11y.ariaExpanded: Communicates whether the dropdown panel is open or closed
a11y.ariaInvalid: Set to true when errorText is present
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links helper or error text to the input for screen reader context
a11y.ariaRequired: Set to true when the required prop is active
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name when no visible label is present
a11y.focusNative: Uses a native input element — participates in the tab order by default
a11y.focusTrap: Focus is trapped inside the dropdown panel while it is open

dateField.title: DateField
dateField.description: Date input with a calendar popover. Localizable, with minDate / maxDate and configurable display format.

seo.metaTitle: Vue Date Picker — Calendar Date Field
seo.metaDescription: Free, open-source Vue date picker: date field with a calendar popover, localizable formatting and minDate/maxDate limits. A Nuxt date picker with zero license cost.
seo.intro: mood-ui's DateField is a free, open-source date picker for Vue 3 and Nuxt — a date input with a calendar popover, localizable display format and minDate/maxDate limits.

basic.title: Basic
basic.desc: Field with label, calendar popover and medium display format by default.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.desc: Available in small, medium and large sharing the input proportions.

minMax.title: Min and max
minMax.desc: Restrict the selectable range using minDate and maxDate.

clearable.title: Clearable
clearable.desc: An ✕ button appears when a date is set to quickly clear the value.

disabled.title: Disabled
disabled.desc: The field dims, blocks the popover and preserves the visible value.

props.label: Visible label above the field.
props.placeholder: Text shown when no date is selected.
props.displayFormat: Intl format used to display the selected date.
props.minDate: Minimum selectable date in the calendar.
props.maxDate: Maximum selectable date in the calendar.
props.clearable: Shows a clear button when a date is set.
props.disabled: Disables the field and prevents opening the popover.
props.readonly: Makes the field non-interactive while preserving the visible value.
props.required: Marks the field as required for validation.
props.fullWidth: The field occupies 100% of the available width.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted when the user selects or clears the date.
emits.open: Emitted when the calendar popover opens.
emits.clear: Emitted when the clear button is pressed.

types.title: DateField
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the DateField component props. Supports date selection with calendar popover, min/max constraints, locale and display format.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.kbEsc: Close the calendar popover without selecting
a11y.kbArrow: Navigate days in the calendar grid
a11y.ariaExpanded: Communicates whether the calendar popover is open or closed
a11y.ariaInvalid: Set to true when errorText is present
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links helper or error text to the trigger for screen reader context
a11y.ariaRequired: Set to true when the required prop is active
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name when no visible label is present
a11y.focusNative: Uses a native button as trigger — participates in the tab order by default
a11y.focusTrap: Focus is trapped inside the calendar popover while it is open

dateRangeField.title: DateRangeField
dateRangeField.description: Date range picker with a dual-calendar popover and preset shortcuts.

basic.title: Basic
basic.desc: Range picker with a dual-calendar popover and medium display format by default.

presets.title: With presets
presets.desc: Enable show-presets to add quick shortcuts like today, this week or this month.

minMax.title: Min and max
minMax.desc: Restrict the selectable range using minDate and maxDate.

disabled.title: Disabled
disabled.desc: The field dims, blocks the popover and preserves the visible range.

props.label: Visible label above the field.
props.placeholder: Text shown when no range is selected.
props.months: Number of months visible in the popover.
props.showPresets: Shows a column with preset shortcuts (today, week, month…).
props.minDate: Minimum selectable date in the calendar.
props.maxDate: Maximum selectable date in the calendar.
props.minRange: Minimum number of days between start and end.
props.maxRange: Maximum number of days between start and end.
props.clearable: Shows a button to clear the range.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted when the range changes.
emits.clear: Emitted when the range is cleared.

types.title: DateRangeField
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the DateRangeField component props. Supports dual-calendar range picker with preset shortcuts, min/max constraints and configurable months.
types.label: Human-readable label shown in the preset list.
types.value: Tuple with the start and end Date of the range.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.kbArrow: Navigate days in the calendar grids
a11y.ariaExpanded: Communicates whether the popover is open or closed
a11y.ariaInvalid: Set to true when errorText is present
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links helper or error text to the trigger for screen reader context
a11y.ariaRequired: Set to true when the required prop is active
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name when no visible label is present
a11y.focusNative: Uses a native button as trigger — participates in the tab order by default
a11y.focusTrap: Focus is trapped inside the popover while it is open

dateTimeField.title: DateTimeField
dateTimeField.description: Combined date and time input with a calendar popover and built-in time picker.

seo.metaTitle: Vue Datetime Picker — Date & Time Field
seo.metaDescription: Free, open-source Vue datetime picker: combined date and time field with a calendar popover and built-in time picker for Vue 3 and Nuxt. Zero license cost.
seo.intro: mood-ui's DateTimeField is a free, open-source datetime picker for Vue 3 and Nuxt — a combined date and time field with a calendar popover and a built-in time picker.

basic.title: Basic
basic.desc: Combined date and time picker using 24-hour format by default.

format12h.title: 12-hour format
format12h.desc: Switch the time picker to AM/PM format with format='12h'.

minMax.title: Min and max
minMax.desc: Restrict the range using minDate and maxDate applied to both date and time.

clearable.title: Clearable
clearable.desc: An ✕ button is visible when a value is set to clear the selection.

disabled.title: Disabled
disabled.desc: The field dims and prevents opening the popover.

timeOnly.title: Time only
timeOnly.desc: Set timeOnly to hide the calendar and show just the time picker. v-model is still Date | null — the date part is set to today when the user picks a time.

props.label: Visible label above the field.
props.placeholder: Text shown when there is no value.
props.step: Minute granularity in the time picker.
props.showSeconds: Shows a seconds column in the time picker.
props.clearable: Shows a button to clear the value.
props.timeOnly: Hides the calendar and shows only the time picker columns.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted when the value changes.
emits.clear: Emitted when the value is cleared.

types.title: DateTimeField
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the DateTimeField component props. Supports combined date and time picker with calendar popover, time format, step control and min/max constraints.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.kbArrow: Navigate days, hours or minutes in the picker
a11y.ariaExpanded: Communicates whether the popover is open or closed
a11y.ariaInvalid: Set to true when errorText is present
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links helper or error text to the trigger for screen reader context
a11y.ariaRequired: Set to true when the required prop is active
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name when no visible label is present
a11y.focusNative: Uses a native button as trigger — participates in the tab order by default
a11y.focusTrap: Focus is trapped inside the popover while it is open

fileInput.title: FileInput
fileInput.description: File selector with drag & drop support, validation, image previews and two visual variants (inline and prominent area).

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.desc: Single-file selection with inline presentation.

multiple.title: Multiple files
multiple.desc: Enable multiple to allow selecting several files at once.

accept.title: Images only
accept.desc: Restrict accepted types with the accept attribute.

dropzone.title: Drag & drop
dropzone.desc: The 'area' variant shows a prominent dropzone with an optional dashed border.

previews.title: With previews
previews.desc: Selected images automatically show a thumbnail.

props.accept: Accepted file type filter (MIME types or extensions).
props.maxSize: Maximum file size per file in bytes.
props.maxFiles: Maximum number of files in multiple mode.
props.dropzone: Enables drag & drop on the area.
props.dropzoneVariant: Presentation style: compact row or prominent dropzone area.
props.dropzoneHeight: Minimum height of the area when dropzoneVariant="area".
props.dashed: Applies a dashed border to the wrapper.
props.dropzoneLabel: Main text of the dropzone area (dropzoneVariant="area" only).
props.dropzoneHint: Secondary text of the dropzone area (dropzoneVariant="area" only).
props.clickToSelect: Clicking anywhere on the area opens the native file picker.
props.label: Visible label above the control.
props.placeholder: Text shown when no files are selected.
props.errorText: Error message that replaces the helperText.
props.color: Semantic color for focus and accent.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted when the file list changes (syncs v-model).
emits.reject: Emitted when a file is rejected due to type, size or count constraints.

types.title: FileInputFile
types.desc: Shape of each file managed by the component. Useful when providing external progress or error maps.
types.name: File name (human-readable).
types.previewUrl: Local object URL for image previews (created via URL.createObjectURL).

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.kbSpace: Open the native file picker (when clickToSelect is true)
a11y.ariaInvalid: Set to true when errorText is present
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links helper or error text to the control for screen reader context
a11y.ariaRequired: Set to true when the required prop is active
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name when no visible label is present
a11y.focusNative: The file input is a native element — participates in the tab order by default
a11y.focusDropzone: The dropzone area is focusable when clickToSelect is enabled

formField.title: FormField
formField.description: Accessible wrapper that provides label, helper text, error state and id/aria linking for any form control.

playground.label: Email
playground.helperText: We will use this to confirm your account.

input.title: With Input
input.desc: Wrapping a text field with label and helper text.

select.title: With Select
select.desc: FormField also works with any composed control.

textarea.title: With Textarea
textarea.desc: Use the hint prop to add auxiliary text to the right of the label.

error.title: With error
error.desc: errorText replaces the helperText and propagates the invalid state to the control.

horizontal.title: Horizontal layout
horizontal.desc: orientation='horizontal' places the label on the left with a fixed width.

props.label: Visible label text. Linked to the control via for/id.
props.helperText: Helper text below the control. Hidden when errorText is present.
props.errorText: Error message. Activates aria-invalid and danger color on the control.
props.required: Shows the red asterisk and propagates required to the slot.
props.disabled: Applies the disabled state and propagates it to the child control.
props.id: id linked to the label. Auto-generated if omitted.
props.orientation: Layout of the label relative to the control.
props.fullWidth: Applies w-full to the outer wrapper.
props.hint: Auxiliary text to the right of the label (e.g. "Optional").
props.as: HTML tag of the root wrapper.

slots.default: Form control. Receives \{ id, describedBy, invalid, disabled, required, color \} as slot props.
slots.label: Replaces the label content when custom HTML is needed.
slots.helper: Replaces the helperText with rich content.

types.title: FormField & FormFieldSlotProps
types.desc: FormField defines the wrapper props. FormFieldSlotProps are the bindings exposed by the default slot to wire aria/ids automatically onto the inner control.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.ariaGroup: The wrapper is exposed as a group for screen readers
a11y.ariaLabelledBy: The label id is linked to the control so screen readers announce the label
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Helper or error text id is linked to the control for additional context
a11y.ariaInvalid: Set to true when errorText is present, marking the field as invalid
a11y.ariaRequired: Set to true when the required prop is active
a11y.focusLabel: Clicking the label moves focus to the associated form control
a11y.focusError: Error messages are programmatically linked to the control via aria-describedby

input.title: Input
input.description: Text input with label, helper, error messages, icons, prefix/suffix and three visual variants.

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.desc: Input with label, placeholder and v-model.
basic.label: Email

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.desc: Three sizes for different UI densities.

props.label: Visible label above the input.
props.helperText: Helper text below the input. Hidden when errorText is present.
props.errorText: Error message that marks the input as invalid.
props.color: Semantic color applied to the focus state.
props.radius: Corner radius. Inherits from ModoProvider when omitted.
props.halo: Persistent halo (ring) style. Inherits from ModoProvider.
props.fullWidth: Applies w-full to the field root.
props.readonly: Read-only: keeps normal style but prevents editing.
props.required: Marks the field as required and shows the indicator.
props.loading: Shows a loader and locks the input during async operations.
props.clearable: Shows an ✕ button to clear the value when there is content.
props.maxLength: Maximum value length. Activates the counter if showCounter.
props.iconLeft: Icon rendered inside the input on the left.
props.iconRight: Icon rendered inside the input on the right.
props.name: HTML name attribute for form submission.
props.ariaLabel: Accessible name when there is no visible label.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted on every value change (syncs v-model).
emits.focus: Emitted when the input receives focus.
emits.blur: Emitted when the input loses focus.
emits.clear: Emitted when the clear button is clicked (clearable).

types.title: Input
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the Input component props. Supports text-like HTML types with full theming and validation helpers.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.kbEnter: Submit the form (when inside a form element)
a11y.ariaInvalid: Set to true when errorText is present
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links helper or error text to the input for screen reader context
a11y.ariaRequired: Set to true when the required prop is active
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name when no visible label is present
a11y.focusNative: Uses a native input element — participates in the tab order by default
a11y.focusClear: The clear button is focusable and keyboard-activatable (Enter or Space)

multiSelect.title: MultiSelect
multiSelect.description: Multi-select with chips, search and bulk selection. Designed for medium-sized lists where users need to pick multiple options.

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.desc: Multi-selection with removable chips.

searchable.title: Searchable and select all
searchable.desc: Combines search with a toggle to select or deselect all at once.

maxChips.title: Collapsed chips
maxChips.desc: When many items are selected, max-visible-chips collapses the rest into a +N badge.

maxSelected.title: Selection limit
maxSelected.desc: max-selected prevents exceeding the specified number of options.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.desc: Three sizes for different form contexts.

props.label: Visible label above the field.
props.placeholder: Text shown when nothing is selected.
props.errorText: Error message that replaces the helperText.
props.color: Semantic color for the control and the chips.
props.radius: Border radius of the trigger and chips.
props.clearable: Shows an X button to clear all selections.
props.searchable: Activates the search input in the panel.
props.showSelectAll: Shows a "Select all" button in the panel.
props.maxVisibleChips: Number of chips before collapsing to "+N".
props.loading: Shows a spinner instead of the chevron.
props.fullWidth: Applies w-full to the root control.
props.iconLeft: Icon rendered to the left of the trigger.
props.name: Generates hidden inputs for form submission.
props.ariaLabel: Accessible name when there is no visible label.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted when the selection array changes.

types.title: MultiSelect
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the MultiSelect component props. Uses SelectOption for its options array. See the Select page for the option shape.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.kbArrow: Navigate between options; Space toggles selection
a11y.ariaExpanded: Communicates whether the dropdown panel is open or closed
a11y.ariaInvalid: Set to true when errorText is present
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links helper or error text to the trigger for screen reader context
a11y.ariaRequired: Set to true when the required prop is active
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name when no visible label is present
a11y.focusNative: Uses a native button as trigger — participates in the tab order by default
a11y.focusTrap: Focus is trapped inside the dropdown panel while it is open

numberInput.title: NumberInput
numberInput.description: Numeric input with steppers, min/max, decimal precision, thousand separator and prefix/suffix.

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.desc: Numeric input with steppers on the right (default).

minMax.title: Min / max + step
minMax.desc: Restrict the value range and the stepper increment.

price.title: Price with prefix and separator
price.desc: Combines decimal precision, decimal step, prefix/suffix and thousand-separator formatting.

split.title: Split stepper
split.desc: Minus on the left and plus on the right of the input.

props.modelValue: Numeric value (v-model). null when the input is empty.
props.label: Visible label above the input.
props.helperText: Helper text. Hidden when errorText is present.
props.errorText: Error message that marks the input as invalid.
props.color: Semantic color applied to the focus state.
props.radius: Corner radius. Inherited from ModoProvider if omitted.
props.readonly: Read-only: hides steppers and blocks editing.
props.precision: Forced decimal places when formatting the display after blur.
props.stepperPosition: Stepper placement: stacked on the right or split (− left, + right).
props.allowInvalid: Allows out-of-range values while typing; clamps on blur.
props.thousandSeparator: Formats the display with a thousand separator after blur.
props.iconLeft: Icon rendered to the left of the input.
props.name: HTML name attribute.
props.ariaLabel: Accessible name when there is no visible label.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted on every value change (syncs v-model).
emits.change: Emitted after a change confirmed by the user.
emits.focus: Emitted when the input receives focus.
emits.blur: Emitted when the input loses focus.

types.title: NumberInput
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the NumberInput component props. Supports steppers, clamping, precision, and thousand separators.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.kbArrow: Increment or decrement the value (when steppers are enabled)
a11y.ariaInvalid: Set to true when the value is out of range or errorText is present
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links helper or error text to the input for screen reader context
a11y.ariaRequired: Set to true when the required prop is active
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name when no visible label is present
a11y.focusNative: Uses a native input element — participates in the tab order by default
a11y.focusSteppers: Stepper buttons are focusable and keyboard-activatable (Enter or Space)

otpInput.title: OTPInput
otpInput.description: One-time password input with configurable digit slots, paste support, auto-advance and mask mode.

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.desc: Six numeric slots. Auto-advances to the next slot on input and supports paste.

separator.title: With separator
separator.desc: A dash is rendered after a given slot index, useful for 6-digit codes split as 3+3.

mask.title: Masked
mask.desc: Set mask to hide the entered characters — useful for PIN entry.

alphanumeric.title: Alphanumeric
alphanumeric.desc: Disable numeric-only mode to accept letters and numbers.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.desc: Three sizes to match form density.

props.numeric: Restrict input to digits only. Set to false for alphanumeric OTPs.
props.separator: Slot index after which a separator dash is rendered.
props.label: Visible label above the slots.
props.helperText: Helper text (hidden when errorText is present).
props.errorText: Error message. Marks the field as invalid when set.
props.color: Semantic color applied to focus ring and accent states.
props.radius: Corner radius for each slot. Inherits from ModoProvider when omitted.
props.fullWidth: Applies w-full to the root element.
props.readonly: Read-only mode: shows values but prevents editing.
props.autofocus: Auto-focus the first slot on mount.
props.name: HTML name attribute for the hidden native input (form submission).
props.ariaLabel: Accessible name for the slot group when no visible label is provided.
props.invalid: Forces the invalid state without rendering errorText.
props.ariaDescribedBy: Extra IDs to concatenate into aria-describedby.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted on every slot change (syncs v-model).
emits.complete: Emitted once all slots are filled.

types.title: OTPInput
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the OTPInput component props.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.kbType: Type a digit (or alphanumeric character) to fill the slot and advance
a11y.kbBackspace: Clear the current slot or move back and clear the previous
a11y.kbArrow: Navigate between slots with ArrowLeft / ArrowRight
a11y.kbPaste: Paste a full OTP code — characters are distributed across slots
a11y.ariaGroup: Slots are wrapped in a role=group with an accessible label
a11y.ariaInvalid: Set to true when errorText is present
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links helper or error text to the group for screen reader context
a11y.ariaLabel: Each slot has its own aria-label (e.g. "Digit 1")
a11y.focusNative: Each slot is a native input — participates in the tab order by default
a11y.autocomplete: The first slot uses autocomplete=one-time-code for SMS auto-fill

passwordInput.title: PasswordInput
passwordInput.description: Password input with a visibility toggle and an optional heuristic strength meter.

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.desc: Visibility toggle enabled by default.
basic.label: Password

strength.title: With strength meter
strength.desc: strengthMeter computes a heuristic score (length + character classes) and displays it below the input.
strength.label: New password

noToggle.title: No visibility toggle
noToggle.desc: Useful when the app controls visibility itself or it does not apply.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.desc: Three sizes aligned with the rest of the inputs.

defaultVisible.title: Visible by default
defaultVisible.desc: Shows the password in plain text on mount. Useful for confirmation fields.

props.label: Visible label above the input.
props.helperText: Helper text. Hidden when errorText is present.
props.errorText: Error message that marks the input as invalid.
props.color: Semantic color applied to the focus state.
props.radius: Corner radius. Inherits from ModoProvider when omitted.
props.maxLength: Maximum length. Activates the counter if showCounter.
props.toggleVisibility: Shows the button to toggle password visibility.
props.defaultVisible: Whether the content starts visible on mount.
props.strengthMeter: Shows a heuristic strength bar below the input.
props.strengthLabels: Custom labels for each strength level (i18n).
props.iconLeft: Icon rendered inside the input on the left.
props.prefix: Fixed text on the left of the input.
props.name: HTML name attribute.
props.ariaLabel: Accessible name when there is no visible label.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted on every value change (syncs v-model).
emits.focus: Emitted when the input receives focus.
emits.blur: Emitted when the input loses focus.
emits.updateVisible: Emitted when the password visibility is toggled.
emits.strengthChange: Emitted when the strength score changes (0–4).

types.title: PasswordInput
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the PasswordInput component props. Includes PasswordStrengthScore and PasswordStrengthLabels for customizing the strength meter.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.kbType: Type text into the password field
a11y.ariaInvalid: Set to true when errorText is present
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links helper, error or strength meter text to the input
a11y.ariaRequired: Set to true when the required prop is active
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name when no visible label is present
a11y.ariaPressed: The visibility toggle button exposes its on/off state
a11y.focusNative: Uses a native input element — participates in the tab order by default
a11y.focusToggle: The visibility toggle button is focusable and keyboard-activatable

phoneInput.title: PhoneInput
phoneInput.description: International phone number field with a searchable country selector, configurable output format and full accessibility support.

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.desc: Phone input with label. Country defaults to US. The number portion is bound via v-model.
basic.label: Phone

country.title: Controlled country
country.desc: Use v-model:country to control the country externally and react to country changes.
country.label: Phone

formats.title: Output formats
formats.desc: The format prop controls what update:modelValue emits. national returns what the user typed, international prepends +{dialCode}, and e164 strips non-digits then prepends +{dialCode}.

variants.title: Variants
variants.desc: outline (default), filled and ghost visual styles.
variants.label: Phone

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.desc: Three field sizes for different UI densities.
sizes.label: Phone

props.modelValue: Phone number value. The format is controlled by the format prop.
props.country: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code for the selected country (v-model:country).
props.defaultCountry: Default country code shown on first render when country is not bound.
props.format: Output format emitted via update:modelValue. national returns the raw typed value; international prepends +{dialCode}; e164 returns digits-only E.164.
props.label: Visible label above the field.
props.placeholder: Placeholder text for the number input.
props.helperText: Helper text below the field. Hidden when errorText is present.
props.errorText: Error message that marks the field as invalid.
props.color: Semantic color applied to the focus state.
props.radius: Corner radius. Inherits from ModoProvider when omitted.
props.halo: Persistent halo (ring) style. Inherits from ModoProvider.
props.fullWidth: Applies w-full to the field root.
props.readonly: Read-only: maintains normal styling but prevents editing.
props.required: Marks the field as required and shows the visual indicator.
props.loading: Shows a spinner and disables the field during async operations.
props.name: HTML name attribute. Submitted as name-local (number part) and name (full formatted value).
props.ariaLabel: Accessible name when no visible label is provided.
props.invalid: Forces invalid state without rendering errorText. Useful inside FormField.
props.ariaDescribedBy: Additional IDs to concatenate into aria-describedby.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted on every input change. Value format is determined by the format prop.
emits.updateCountry: Emitted when the user selects a new country. Payload is the ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code.
emits.focus: Emitted when the number input receives focus.
emits.blur: Emitted when the number input loses focus.

types.title: PhoneInput & PhoneCountry
types.desc: TypeScript interfaces for the PhoneInput component props and the internal country data shape.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus between the country selector button, the number input and the next field
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.kbEnterSpace: Open the country dropdown when the selector button is focused
a11y.kbArrowDown: Move to the next country in the dropdown list
a11y.kbArrowUp: Move to the previous country in the dropdown list
a11y.kbEnterSelect: Select the highlighted country in the dropdown
a11y.kbEscape: Close the country dropdown and return focus to the trigger button
a11y.kbType: Type in the search box to filter countries by name, ISO code or dial code
a11y.ariaInvalid: Set to true on the phone input when errorText is present
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links helper or error text to the input for screen reader context
a11y.ariaRequired: Set to true when the required prop is active
a11y.ariaExpanded: Reflects open/closed state of the country dropdown on the trigger button
a11y.ariaHasPopup: Set to listbox on the country selector trigger to announce the dropdown type
a11y.ariaLabel: The country selector button always has an aria-label ('Select country' by default)
a11y.focusComposite: The field is a composite: the country button and the tel input are separate focusable elements
a11y.focusDropdown: On opening, focus moves to the search input inside the dropdown for immediate filtering

radio.title: Radio
radio.description: Individual radio button. For groups, prefer RadioGroup which manages roving tabindex and shares the name attribute automatically.

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.desc: Three radios sharing the same v-model. Only one can be selected at a time.

description.title: With description
description.desc: Secondary text to add context to each option.
description.freeDesc: For personal use with basic features.
description.proDesc: For small teams with advanced features.

colors.title: Colors
colors.desc: Semantic variants for the checked state.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.desc: Three sizes to match the form density.

disabled.title: Disabled
disabled.desc: Disabled state preserving the visual value.

props.modelValue: Selected value of the group (v-model). Compared against value to determine checked.
props.value: Value emitted by this radio when selected.
props.label: Visible label to the right of the control.
props.description: Secondary descriptive text below the label.
props.color: Semantic color applied in the checked state.
props.size: Size of the control and the label.
props.disabled: Disables the radio and prevents interaction.
props.name: HTML name attribute. Inherited automatically when inside a RadioGroup.
props.ariaLabel: Accessible name when there is no visible label.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted when this radio is selected (syncs v-model with its value).

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.kbArrow: Move selection between radios in the same group
a11y.ariaInvalid: Set to true when errorText is present
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links helper or error text to the radio for screen reader context
a11y.ariaRequired: Set to true when the required prop is active
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name when no visible label is present
a11y.focusNative: Uses a native input[type=radio] — participates in the tab order by default
a11y.focusGroup: Arrow keys cycle between radios sharing the same name attribute

radioGroup.title: RadioGroup
radioGroup.description: Radio group with single selection, semantic fieldset/legend and full label, helper and error support.

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.desc: Option list with single selection bound via v-model.
basic.label: Plan

described.title: With description
described.desc: Each option can include secondary text to add context.
described.label: Choose your plan

horizontal.title: Horizontal
horizontal.desc: Ideal for short options aligned in a single row.
horizontal.label: Favourite color

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.desc: Three sizes available for different UI densities.

disabled.title: Disabled
disabled.desc: Locks the entire group while keeping the selection visible.
disabled.label: Disabled

props.options: Options \{ value, label, description?, disabled? \} to render.
props.name: Name attribute shared by all radios. Auto-generated if omitted.
props.label: Group label (rendered as a legend element).
props.orientation: Layout direction of the radios within the group.
props.color: Semantic color applied to all radios.
props.ariaLabel: Accessible name when there is no visible label.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted when the selected option changes (syncs v-model).

slots.default: Allows rendering Radios manually when per-option customisation is needed.

types.title: RadioOption
types.desc: Shape of each item in the options array.
types.value: Unique identifier used for v-model comparison. Must be a string or number.
types.label: Text rendered next to the radio control.
types.description: Secondary text rendered below the label.
types.disabled: When true, the option is visible but not selectable.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus into or out of the radio group
a11y.kbArrow: Move selection between radios within the group
a11y.ariaGroup: The wrapper is exposed as a radiogroup for screen readers
a11y.ariaLabelledBy: The group label is linked so screen readers announce the group context
a11y.ariaInvalid: Set to true when errorText is present
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links helper or error text to the group for screen reader context
a11y.ariaRequired: Set to true when the required prop is active
a11y.focusGroup: Arrow keys cycle between radios; Tab moves focus out of the group
a11y.focusDisabled: Disabled radios remain in the arrow cycle but cannot be selected

rangeSlider.title: RangeSlider
rangeSlider.description: Dual-thumb slider for selecting a numeric range. Supports marks, value formatting and the usual visual modes.

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.desc: Range selection with two thumbs and v-model as a tuple.
basic.label: Range

marks.title: With marks
marks.desc: Marks at regular intervals to guide selection.
marks.label: Age

formatted.title: Formatted values
formatted.desc: formatValue lets you display units, prefixes or any custom format.
formatted.label: Price

colors.title: Colors
colors.desc: Semantic variants aligned with the rest of the system.

disabled.title: Disabled
disabled.desc: Disabled state preserving the visible values.
disabled.label: Disabled

props.modelValue: Tuple [from, to] with the range values (v-model).
props.minDistance: Minimum distance between the two thumbs.
props.showValue: Displays the current range next to the label.
props.formatValue: Function to format the displayed values.
props.label: Visible label above the control.
props.color: Semantic color of the filled segment and thumbs.
props.readonly: Locks changes while keeping values visible.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted when the range changes (syncs v-model).
emits.change: Emitted when a drag or keyboard interaction ends.

types.title: RangeSlider
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the RangeSlider component props. Extends Slider with dual-thumb range selection and minimum distance constraint.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus between the two thumbs
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.kbArrow: Increment or decrement the focused thumb by one step
a11y.kbHome: Jump the focused thumb to the minimum value
a11y.kbEnd: Jump the focused thumb to the maximum value
a11y.ariaValueNow: Each thumb announces its current numeric value
a11y.ariaInvalid: Set to true when errorText is present
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links helper or error text to the slider for screen reader context
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name when no visible label is present
a11y.focusThumbs: Both thumbs are independently focusable and keyboard-operable

rating.title: Rating
rating.description: Star rating control. Supports half steps, read-only mode and all color and size variants.

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.desc: Five stars, warning color by default. Clicking the active value resets it.

half.title: Half stars
half.desc: Enable allow-half for ratings in 0.5 steps and show the numeric value.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.desc: Three sizes available.

readonlyDisabled.title: Read-only and disabled
readonlyDisabled.desc: readonly preserves active styles; disabled adds opacity.

props.clearable: Re-clicking the active value resets it to 0.
props.color: Semantic color of the filled stars.
props.showValue: Displays the numeric value next to the rating.
props.ariaLabel: Accessible name.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted when the value changes (syncs v-model).

types.title: Rating
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the Rating component props. Supports half-steps, clearable stars, and numeric value display.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.kbArrow: Change the rating value by one step
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name for the rating group
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.focusNative: Each star is a focusable button — arrow keys navigate and change the value
a11y.focusReadonly: Read-only mode preserves visual state and announces the current value without allowing edits

searchInput.title: SearchInput
searchInput.description: Search-optimised input: magnifier icon, automatic clear button, optional debounce and keyboard shortcut support.

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.desc: Magnifier icon and automatic ✕ button when there is a value.

debounce.title: With debounce
debounce.desc: Emits the search event after the specified delay. Useful for API calls.

shortcut.title: With shortcut
shortcut.desc: Shows a kbd hint on the right. The combination focuses the input globally.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.desc: Three sizes aligned with the rest of the inputs.

loading.title: Loading
loading.desc: Shows a spinner instead of the clear button while searching.

props.label: Visible label above the input.
props.helperText: Helper text. Hidden when errorText is present.
props.errorText: Error message that marks the input as invalid.
props.color: Semantic color applied to the focus state.
props.radius: Corner radius. Inherits from ModoProvider when omitted.
props.loading: Shows a spinner instead of the clear button.
props.maxLength: Maximum length. Activates the counter if showCounter.
props.debounce: Delay (ms) before emitting 'search' while typing. 0 = only emits on Enter.
props.shortcut: Global keyboard shortcut that focuses the input. Shown as a kbd hint (e.g. 'cmd+k').
props.name: HTML name attribute.
props.ariaLabel: Accessible name when there is no visible label.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted on every value change (syncs v-model).
emits.focus: Emitted when the input receives focus.
emits.blur: Emitted when the input loses focus.
emits.clear: Emitted when the clear button is clicked.
emits.search: Emitted on Enter or after the debounce delay.

types.title: SearchInput
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the SearchInput component props. Includes debounce and global keyboard shortcut support.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.kbEsc: Clear the current value when clearable
a11y.ariaSearch: The input is exposed with role search for semantic context
a11y.ariaInvalid: Set to true when errorText is present
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links helper or error text to the input for screen reader context
a11y.ariaRequired: Set to true when the required prop is active
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name when no visible label is present
a11y.focusNative: Uses a native input element — participates in the tab order by default
a11y.focusShortcut: Global keyboard shortcut (e.g. Cmd+K) moves focus directly to the search input

segmented.title: Segmented
segmented.description: Single-value pill-group selector with a sliding indicator. Ideal for switching views or short filters.

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.desc: Three segments with labels only.

icons.title: With icons
icons.desc: Each segment can have an optional icon to the left of the label.

colors.title: Colors
colors.desc: Semantic variants applied to the text and indicator of the active segment.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.desc: Three heights for different UI densities.

fullWidth.title: Full width
fullWidth.desc: With full-width the track fills the container and segments share the width equally.

props.items: Segment list \{ value, label?, icon?, disabled?, ariaLabel? \}.
props.color: Semantic color applied to the active segment.
props.radius: Corner radius of the track and segments.
props.fullWidth: Stretches the control to fill the container; segments share the width.
props.ariaLabel: Accessible name for the radiogroup.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted when the active segment changes (syncs v-model).

types.title: SegmentedItem
types.desc: Shape of each segment in the items array. Import it to type your data before passing it to the component.
types.value: Unique value emitted via update:modelValue when selected. Must be a string or number.
types.label: Visible label. Optional when an icon is provided.
types.icon: Optional leading icon rendered to the left of the label.
types.disabled: When true, the segment is visible but not selectable.
types.ariaLabel: Override the accessible name. Required when label is omitted.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus into or out of the segmented control
a11y.ariaGroup: The wrapper is exposed as a radiogroup for screen readers
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name for the radiogroup
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.focusGroup: Arrow keys cycle between segments; Tab moves focus out of the group
a11y.focusDisabled: Disabled segments remain visible but are skipped by keyboard navigation

select.title: Select
select.description: Accessible single-value selector with a dropdown panel. Supports search, grouping, icons and the standard visual modes.

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.desc: Simple selector with static options and a placeholder.
basic.label: Country

searchable.title: Searchable
searchable.desc: Enable searchable to show a search input inside the panel.
searchable.label: Language

grouped.title: Grouped options
grouped.desc: Options sharing the same group property are rendered under a heading.
grouped.label: Language

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.desc: Three sizes to match form density.

states.title: States
states.desc: Loading, disabled and error with inline feedback.

props.modelValue: Selected value (v-model). null when nothing is selected.
props.options: Option list \{ value, label, description?, icon?, disabled?, group? \}.
props.label: Visible label above the field.
props.placeholder: Text shown when there is no selected value.
props.color: Semantic color for focus and accent.
props.radius: Corner radius of the trigger and panel.
props.clearable: Shows an X button to clear the selection.
props.searchable: Shows a search input inside the panel.
props.loading: Shows a spinner instead of the chevron.
props.fullWidth: Applies w-full to the control root.
props.iconLeft: Icon rendered to the left of the trigger.
props.panelWidth: Calculates panel width from the trigger or its content.
props.name: HTML name attribute for form submission.
props.ariaLabel: Accessible name when there is no visible label.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted when the value changes (syncs v-model).

types.title: SelectOption
types.desc: Shape of each item in the options array. Import it when you want to type your data before passing it to the component.
types.value: Unique identifier. Must be a string or number.
types.label: Text shown in the trigger and in the dropdown item.
types.description: Secondary text rendered below the label inside the dropdown item.
types.icon: Optional icon rendered to the left of the label.
types.disabled: When true, the item is visible but not selectable.
types.group: Group name — items with the same group are visually grouped together.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.kbArrow: Navigate between options inside the panel
a11y.ariaExpanded: Communicates whether the dropdown panel is open or closed
a11y.ariaInvalid: Set to true when errorText is present
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links helper or error text to the trigger for screen reader context
a11y.ariaRequired: Set to true when the required prop is active
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name when no visible label is present
a11y.focusNative: Uses a native button as trigger — participates in the tab order by default
a11y.focusTrap: Focus is trapped inside the dropdown panel while it is open

slider.title: Slider
slider.description: Single-value numeric slider with marks, value formatting, label and all the usual visual variants.

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.desc: Slider with visible value and label.
basic.label: Volume

marks.title: With marks
marks.desc: Enable marks to show reference points along the track.
marks.label: Quality

step.title: Custom step
step.desc: Control the granularity of the slider with the step prop.
step.label: Step of 5

disabled.title: Disabled
disabled.desc: Disabled state preserving the visible value.
disabled.label: Disabled

props.showValue: Displays the numeric value next to the label.
props.formatValue: Function to format the displayed value.
props.marks: Discrete marks. true = one per step.
props.label: Visible label above the control.
props.color: Semantic color of the fill and thumb.
props.readonly: Locks changes while keeping the value visible.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted when the value changes (syncs v-model).
emits.change: Emitted when a drag or keyboard interaction ends.

types.title: Slider
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the Slider component props. Supports marks, custom formatting, vertical orientation, and value tooltips.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.kbArrow: Increment or decrement the value by one step
a11y.ariaValueNow: The current numeric value is announced to screen readers
a11y.ariaInvalid: Set to true when errorText is present
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links helper or error text to the slider for screen reader context
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name when no visible label is present
a11y.focusThumb: The thumb is focusable and keyboard-operable; arrow keys change the value

switch.title: Switch
switch.description: Semantically accessible on/off toggle. Supports left or right label position, description and all color and size variants.

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.desc: Binary toggle with v-model and a visible label.

description.title: With description
description.desc: Secondary text below the label to add context.
description.label: Marketing

labelLeft.title: Label on the left
labelLeft.desc: Useful in settings rows with the control aligned to the right.
labelLeft.label: Active

colors.title: Colors
colors.desc: Semantic variants for the on state.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.desc: Three sizes available for different UI densities.

disabled.title: Disabled
disabled.desc: Disabled state preserving the visual value.

props.label: Visible label next to the control.
props.description: Secondary descriptive text below the label.
props.helperText: Helper text below the control. Hidden when errorText is present.
props.errorText: Error message that replaces helperText and applies error state.
props.color: Semantic color applied in the on state.
props.radius: Track radius. The thumb remains circular.
props.labelPosition: Position of the label relative to the control.
props.disabled: Disables the switch and prevents interaction.
props.readonly: Locks changes but keeps focus and active styles.
props.required: Marks the field as required and shows an asterisk.
props.name: HTML name attribute for form submission.
props.ariaLabel: Accessible name when there is no visible label.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted when the value changes (syncs v-model).

types.title: Switch
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the Switch component props. Includes label positioning and full semantic color support.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.ariaChecked: Communicates on or off state to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaInvalid: Set to true when errorText is present
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links helper or error text to the switch for screen reader context
a11y.ariaRequired: Set to true when the required prop is active
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name when no visible label is present
a11y.focusNative: Uses a native button element — participates in the tab order by default

tagInput.title: TagInput
tagInput.description: Tag editor: the user types text, presses Enter (or a delimiter) and a chip is added. Supports validation, maximums and visual variants.

playground.placeholder: Add a tag and press Enter…

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.desc: Press Enter or comma to confirm. Duplicates are discarded by default.
basic.ph: Add a tag and press Enter…

max.title: Maximum tags
max.desc: Limit the number of tags with the max prop.

validator.title: Custom validator
validator.desc: Only tags that pass the validator are confirmed (here, valid emails).

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.desc: Three heights to match the form density.

props.placeholder: Placeholder when there are no tags and the input is empty.
props.max: Maximum number of tags. Beyond the limit the input is disabled.
props.validator: Optional validator. Return false to reject the tag.
props.delimiters: Characters that also confirm the current tag when typed.
props.color: Semantic color of the chips.
props.size: Size of the input and chips.
props.radius: Corner radius of the wrapper and chips.
props.readonly: Read-only mode — tags cannot be removed.
props.ariaLabel: Accessible name for the input.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted when the tag list changes (v-model).
emits.add: Emitted when a new tag is added.
emits.remove: Emitted when a tag is removed, with its value and original index.
emits.change: Emitted after any change (add or remove).

types.title: TagInput
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the TagInput component props. Supports custom validators, delimiters, duplicate prevention, and chip theming.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.kbType: Type a tag name and press Enter or a delimiter to add it
a11y.kbBackspace: Remove the last tag when the input is empty
a11y.ariaInvalid: Set to true when errorText is present
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links helper or error text to the input for screen reader context
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name when no visible label is present
a11y.focusNative: Uses a native input element — participates in the tab order by default
a11y.focusChips: Each tag chip has a remove button that is focusable and keyboard-activatable

textarea.title: Textarea
textarea.description: Multi-line text area with autoresize, character counter, helper/error text and all the visual variants of Input.

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.desc: Simple textarea with label, placeholder and initial rows.
basic.label: Comment

autoresize.title: Autoresize + counter
autoresize.desc: Grows with content up to maxRows and shows a character counter.
autoresize.label: Bio

error.title: Error
error.desc: errorText marks the field as invalid and replaces helperText.
error.label: Description

disabled.title: Disabled
disabled.desc: Disabled state preserving the visual value.

resize.title: Resize
resize.desc: Control the browser's manual resize handle with none, vertical, horizontal or both.

props.label: Visible label above the textarea.
props.helperText: Helper text below the textarea. Hidden when errorText is present.
props.errorText: Error message that marks the textarea as invalid.
props.color: Semantic color applied to the focus state.
props.radius: Corner radius. Inherits from ModoProvider when omitted.
props.clearable: Shows an ✕ button to clear the value.
props.maxLength: Maximum length. Activates the counter if showCounter.
props.autoresize: Grows automatically with content up to maxRows.
props.name: HTML name attribute.
props.ariaLabel: Accessible name when there is no visible label.

emits.updateModelValue: Emitted on every value change (syncs v-model).
emits.focus: Emitted when the textarea receives focus.
emits.blur: Emitted when the textarea loses focus.
emits.clear: Emitted when the clear button is clicked (clearable).

types.title: Textarea
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the Textarea component props. Supports autoresize, manual resize control, and character counter.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.ariaInvalid: Set to true when errorText is present
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links helper or error text to the textarea for screen reader context
a11y.ariaRequired: Set to true when the required prop is active
a11y.ariaDisabled: Communicates disabled state to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name when no visible label is present
a11y.focusNative: Uses a native textarea element — participates in the tab order by default
a11y.focusResize: Manual resize handle is a browser feature and does not interfere with keyboard navigation
